ddt:Target.Name: CMF1ph2_REPS_V3.4
ddt:Target.Description:
********************************************* REPS CMF1 ph2 - DAE crémaillère CMF1 ph2 ********************************************** 27/09/2018 "CMF1ph2_REPS_v3.1" new DID added ($01D0) updated DID ($0172: RW) new DTC $5612 $61 added
30/08/2018 "CMF1ph2_REPS_v3"
Update DTC & DID in EPS Screens updates
16/02/2018 "CMF1ph2_REPS_v2" Updated Diag Version to "$26" distributed for the REPS of CMF1ph2 projects (XFC, XFD, BFB GT/RS)ph2 and XFK Add new S2RE03CB03 in Auto-Ident list Remove applicability for XFAph2 project which remains same as XFAph1 Update DTC & DID in EPS Screens updates need to be updated with next SW - DID $0100, $0104, $011D, $0185
24/11/2017 "CMF1ph2_REPS_V1_13" Add SWCB02 in the Auto-Ident list Add SWCC00 with updated VDig in the Auto-Ident list
18/02/2019 "CMF1ph2_REPS_V3.2" Added SWCD00 with the Auto-Ident list
Creation of database based on CMF1 ph1 version "CMF1_REPS_V1_12" Add new DIDs : $01B0, $01C0, $01C2, $01C4, $01CB, $01DB, $01DC, $01E4 Add new DTC : $561F, $5615, $5620 Add new features in screen "Configuration/ 3- Activate functions" Add new screen for TJP counter
14/02/2019 - BD : "CMF1ph2_REPS_V3.3" Added/Corrected new DIDs : $01 00 $01 86 $01 87 $01 88 $01 89 $01 83 $01 90 $01 92 $01 93 $01 94 $01 97 $01 98 $01 99 $02 00 $01 74 Updated all DTC with OBD ref (same as DTC)
17/04/2019 - BD : "CMF1ph2_REPS_V3.4" Updated DID Estimated Offset ($0188) with correct formula (A : 0,1 => 0,01) Updated Parameters with new Auto Ident (CD01)

Item.Text: Device and failure type ODB codding
FM (Frequency Modulated) / PWM (Pulse Width Modulated) Failures
circuit short to ground or open
circuit short to battery or open
signal rate of change below threshold
signal rate of change above threshold
signal bias level out of range / zero adjustment failure
signal has too few transitions / events
signal has too many transitions / events
alive / sequence counter incorrect / not updated
value of signal protection calculation incorrect
Component or system operation obstructed or blocked
FM (Frequency Modulated) / PWM (Pulse Width Modulated) Failures

Data.Name: TuningNumberSelected
Data.Comment: cf. Spec DiagOnCAN: TUNING NUMBER SELECTED

Data.Name: DeratingAssistanceLevel
Data.Comment: cf. Spec DiagOnCAN: Derating assistance level (0 to 100%)

Data.Name: AssistanceLevel
Data.Comment: cf. Spec DiagOnCAN: ASSISTANCE_LEVEL/Percentage of assistance avaliable

Data.Name: SystemMode
Data.Comment: cf. Spec DiagOnCAN: system mode (renault stateflow)

Data.Name: SupplyVoltage
Data.Comment: cf. Spec DiagOnCAN: VOLTAGE_ST/Computer supply voltage

Data.Name: DTCFormatIdentifier
Data.Comment: DTC format - Renault/Nissan use value 0 (3 bytes DTC)

Data.Name: systemSupplierIdentifier
Data.Comment: Name and address information of the supplier.
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier name and address information.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: ECUSerialNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the ECU (server) serial number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific.

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerKitAssemblyPartNumber
Data.Comment: Ordered Part Number - This value shall contain the number of the part ordered by OEM.
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer order number for a kit (assembled parts bought as a whole for production e.g. cockpit), when the spare part number designates only the server (e.g. for aftersales).

The record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: systemSupplierECUSoftwareVersionNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) software version number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerECUHardwareNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used by reading services to reference the vehicle-manufacturer-specific ECU (server) hardware number.

Record data content and format shall be serverspecific and defined by vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: systemSupplierECUSoftwareNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) software number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: SessionName
Data.Comment: Diagnostic session values. See documentation for more information.
Data.Description:
[0x00] : ISO / SAE Reserved
[0x01] : Default session
[0x02] : Programming session
[0x03] : Extended diagnostic session
[0x04] : Safety system diagnostic session
[0x05 - 0x3F] : ISO / SAE Reserved
[0x40 - 0x5F] : Vehicle manufacturer specific
[0x60 - 0x7E] : System supplier specific
[0x7F] : ISO / SAE Reserved

Data.Name: VehicleSpeed
Data.Comment: cf. Spec DiagOnCAN: VEHICULE_SPEED/Vehicle speed (in km/h) from CAN signal

Data.Name: SupplierInternalFaultCode_A.Hex
Data.Comment: Supplier fault code for this DTC (A-fault)

Data.Name: SupplierInternalFaultCode_Event.Hex
Data.Comment: Supplier fault code for this DTC (Event)

Data.Name: SupplierInternalFaultCode_B.Hex
Data.Comment: cf. Spec DiagOnCAN: INTERNAL_FAULT_CODE/Supplier fault code for this DTC

Item.Text: Activated (default value) => if TJP on, EPS shall switch to dedicated TJP mode
Deactivated => if TJP on, EPS follows current selected driving mode

Data.Name: diagnosticVersion
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the diagnostic version of the ECU.

It is defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Refer to manufacture ECU designer.

Data.Name: MotorTemperature
Data.Comment: cf. Spec DiagOnCAN: Motor_TEMPERATURE/Value of the temperature sensor

Data.Name: ElectricalMotorSpeed
Data.Comment: cf spec DiagONCAN Electrical Moto rSpeed

Data.Name: ECUTemperature
Data.Comment: cf. Spec DiagOnCAN: ECU_TEMPERATURE/Value of the temperature sensor

Data.Name: FilteredVehicleSpeed
Data.Comment: cf. Spec DiagOnCAN: FILTERED VEHICLE SPEED/HEPS calculation ; information in use

Item.Text: 4 wheel steering not present (no emission of the frame EPS_R5)

Data.Name: DecoBatt_Activation
Data.Comment: configuration of presence of Deco Batt function

Data.Name: CalibrationNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer specific ECU application data identification record.

The format of the application data identification structure shall be ECU specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Item.Text: Cut off assistance permanently during stop auto (default value)
Cut off assistance with possibility to restart engine by EPS during stop auto
No cut off assistance during stop auto
Only faults reports and alerts that MUST remain active during cranking or sleeping periods are enabled
All faults reports and alerts not related to APC power level are enabled
All faults reports and alerts are enabled

Data.Name: MeasuredMotorCurrent
Data.Comment: non implémentée donc à $00 dans la snapshot

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession.Default
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession.Programming
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession.ExtendedDiagnostic
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Request.Name: ClearDiagnosticInformation.All
Request.Comment: The ClearDiagnosticInformation service is used by the tool to clear diagnostic information in an ECUs memory

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportDTC
Request.Comment: DDT2000 Failures page requests / Clip import : Unusable in the standard tools of DDT2000

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportSnapshot
Request.Comment: DDT2000 Failures page requests / Clip import : Unusable in the standard tools of DDT2000

Request.Name: TesterPresent.WithResponse
Request.Comment: This request enable P2P tester present for tool (Screen, IOctl, ...)

Label.Text: To write DID it is necessary to have no assist: IGN ON, engine stopped, CAN vehicule speed valide <3km/h. ECU reset or IGNOFF/ON is necessary if assistance has been raised already.
Shall be = 2; if not, perform ECU reset or IGN OFF/ON
TJP_MEX DID will work from SW RE03CC00
To write DID it is necessary to have no assist: IGN ON, engine stopped, CAN vehicule speed valide <3km/h. ECU reset or IGNOFF/ON is necessary if assistance has been raised already.
Shall be = 2; if not, perform ECU reset or IGN OFF/ON
DID will work with SW from RE03CC00
DID will work with SW from RE03CC01
These codes are mainly related to CAN or voltage related faults
Values are coded in Hexa 1 fault (on 1 byte)
Supplier Fault Store can be erased only with CANapé (not DDT2000)
Otherwise it means a major EPS fault occured (loss of assistance) => Please send a copy of this screen to EDL
"20" means a ClearDTC has been performed
